hero.headline: ARKA JAIN University Online MBA in Human Resource Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved 2-year programme | NAAC A accredited | Rs 1,08,500 total fee | Generative AI in Sem 1

sections.tldr: ARKA JAIN University Online MBA in Human Resource Management covers Talent Acquisition and Management, Learning and Development, Industrial Relations and Labor Laws, Employee Management and Negotiation Skills, International HRM, Performance Management Systems, and Organization Development and Change Management. Total fee Rs 1,08,500. Zero-cost EMI available directly through the university. NAAC A accredited. UGC-DEB approved. Generative AI for Online Learners is a mandatory Sem 1 subject. ARKA JAIN is a Jharkhand-based JGI Online institution, not JAIN Deemed University (Bengaluru).

about.body:
ARKA JAIN University's Online MBA in Human Resource Management covers the full HR lifecycle: talent acquisition, learning and development, industrial relations, performance management, compensation design, and organizational change. At Rs 1,08,500 total, this is a budget-tier NAAC A online MBA with a distinctive AI component.

IMPORTANT: ARKA JAIN University is a Jharkhand-based institution under the JGI Group, operating from Jamshedpur. It is NOT the same as JAIN Deemed-to-be University in Bengaluru. Different university, different management, different accreditation profile. JAIN Bengaluru is NAAC A++ with NIRF top-100. ARKA JAIN is NAAC A, not ranked in NIRF 2025. Confirm you are applying to the correct institution before proceeding.

The Generative AI for Online Learners course in Semester 1 is a mandatory credit-bearing subject across all MBA specialisations. For HR professionals, this means applied skills in AI-assisted recruitment screening, automated report generation, and prompt engineering for HR analytics. This is built into the curriculum, not a marketing claim.

EdifyEdu verifies data independently and takes no commission from any university.

syllabus.note: Semesters 1-2 cover the common MBA core including Human Resource Management, Business Economics, Sustainability and Ethics, Financial Reporting and Corporate Finance, Organizational Behavior, Quantitative Techniques, Generative AI for Online Learners (Sem 1), and Entrepreneurship, Marketing Management, Operations Research, Business Law and Corporate Governance, Business Analytics and Artificial Intelligence (Sem 2). Semesters 3-4 below show the HRM specialisation subjects plus common subjects.

subjects.name: Business Research Methods
subjects.desc: Research design, data collection methods, sampling techniques, hypothesis testing, and research report writing for management.

subjects.name: Operations Management
subjects.desc: Production planning, supply chain basics, quality management systems, inventory control, and process optimisation.

subjects.name: Talent Acquisition and Management
subjects.desc: Recruitment strategy design, employer branding, candidate assessment frameworks, onboarding processes, and talent pipeline management.

subjects.name: Learning and Development
subjects.desc: Training needs analysis, instructional design, e-learning strategy, competency mapping, and ROI measurement for L&D programmes.

subjects.name: Industrial Relations and Labor Laws
subjects.desc: Trade union management, collective bargaining, Industrial Disputes Act, Factories Act, new Labour Codes, and workplace compliance.

subjects.name: Employee Management and Negotiation Skills
subjects.desc: Conflict resolution, grievance handling, negotiation strategies, employee engagement frameworks, and workplace communication.

subjects.name: Open Elective Course
subjects.desc: Elective chosen from outside the HRM specialisation. Provides cross-functional exposure in finance, marketing, or general management.

subjects.name: Strategic Management
subjects.desc: Competitive strategy frameworks, corporate-level strategy, strategic analysis tools (SWOT, Porter's Five Forces), and strategic implementation.

subjects.name: International Human Resource Management
subjects.desc: Cross-cultural management, expatriate policies, global mobility, international labour standards, and multinational HR strategy.

subjects.name: Performance Management Systems
subjects.desc: KPI design, balanced scorecard, 360-degree feedback, OKR frameworks, performance appraisal methods, and linking performance to rewards.

subjects.name: Organization Development and Change Management
subjects.desc: OD interventions, change readiness assessment, Kotter's model, resistance management, and culture transformation planning.

subjects.name: Employee Compensation and Benefits Management
subjects.desc: Salary structuring, CTC components, variable pay design, ESOP frameworks, statutory benefits, and total rewards strategy.

subjects.name: Master Thesis / Project
subjects.desc: Supervised research project on an HR management topic. Literature review, methodology design, data analysis, and thesis presentation.

whoHires.body:
ARKA JAIN MBA HRM graduates target roles across corporate HR, talent management, and organizational development:

**Industries:** IT/ITES, manufacturing, BFSI, healthcare, retail, e-commerce, consulting, and government/PSU HR departments

**Entry-level roles:** HR Executive, Talent Acquisition Specialist, L&D Coordinator, Payroll Analyst, Employee Engagement Associate

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** HR Manager, Talent Acquisition Manager, L&D Manager, Compensation and Benefits Manager, HR Business Partner

**Senior roles (7+ years):** Head of HR, CHRO (mid-market), VP People Operations, Director of Talent, Head of Organizational Development

**Regional note:** ARKA JAIN's JGI Group network provides stronger placement access in East India (Jharkhand, Bihar, Odisha, West Bengal). Manufacturing and mining sector HR roles are particularly relevant in the Jamshedpur industrial corridor. Candidates targeting metro markets should supplement with independent networking.

skills.body:
The HRM specialisation builds skills across talent management, labour compliance, and organizational development:

**Technical skills:**
- Talent Acquisition: recruitment strategy design, employer branding, candidate assessment, ATS management, and onboarding process design
- Compensation Design: CTC structuring, variable pay models, ESOP frameworks, statutory benefits calculation, and total rewards benchmarking
- Labour Law Compliance: Industrial Disputes Act, Factories Act, new Labour Codes (2020), PF/ESI compliance, and workplace safety norms
- Performance Management: KPI/OKR design, balanced scorecard implementation, 360-degree feedback setup, and appraisal calibration

**Applied skills:**
- Organizational Development: change readiness assessment, culture audit, OD intervention design, and transformation project management
- International HRM: expatriate policy design, cross-cultural team management, global mobility processes, and international labour standards

**AI skills (Sem 1):** Prompt engineering for HR analytics, AI-assisted screening tools, automated report generation, and GenAI for employee communication drafts.

comparisons.body:
Comparing ARKA JAIN Online MBA HRM against peer programmes:

**vs SMU Online MBA HRM (Rs 1,20,000):** SMU is Rs 11,500 more expensive with the established Manipal Group brand and stronger pan-India placement infrastructure. ARKA JAIN adds mandatory GenAI in Sem 1 and is cheaper. Choose SMU for national brand recognition and career services depth. Choose ARKA JAIN for lower fee and AI-integrated curriculum.

**vs Uttaranchal Online MBA HR (Rs 94,000):** Uttaranchal is Rs 14,500 cheaper with NAAC A+ accreditation. Uttaranchal has North India (Dehradun) positioning. ARKA JAIN has Employee Management and Negotiation Skills as a standalone subject plus GenAI in Sem 1. Choose Uttaranchal for lower fee and stronger accreditation grade. Choose ARKA JAIN for East India proximity and negotiation/GenAI curriculum depth.

**vs Galgotias Online MBA HR (Rs 86,400):** Galgotias is Rs 22,100 cheaper with NAAC A+ and NCR location advantage. Both are budget-tier programmes. Galgotias wins on fee and accreditation. ARKA JAIN wins on GenAI integration and the Industrial Relations and Labor Laws standalone subject. Choose based on your geographic base and curriculum priority.

fees.body:
The ARKA JAIN University Online MBA in Human Resource Management is priced at Rs 1,08,500 effective total across 4 semesters (2-year programme). This includes all charges - tuition, registration, and examination fees.

**Fee breakdown**:
- Per semester: Rs 25,000 x 4 semesters = Rs 1,00,000 tuition
- Registration fee: Rs 2,500 one-time
- Examination fee: Rs 3,000 per year x 2 years = Rs 6,000

**Payment options**:
- Semester-wise: Rs 25,000 per semester (plus reg + exam fees at admission)
- Upfront: Rs 1,08,500 one-time inclusive

**Zero-cost EMI**: Available directly through ARKA JAIN University (no external marketing or NBFC partner). Contact our counsellor for current EMI tenure options.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with ARKA JAIN each quarter. Please reconfirm with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body:
Graduates receive an MBA degree certificate with Human Resource Management as the specialisation, issued by ARKA JAIN University under UGC-DEB 2020 online mode standards.

The degree carries NAAC A accreditation and UGC-DEB approval. Valid for government recruitment, PSU HR roles, corporate HR positions, and higher education admissions. The certificate reads Master of Business Administration without the word 'online' on the document face, consistent with UGC-DEB norms. DigiLocker integration available for digital verification.

reviews.intro: Three reviews from ARKA JAIN Online MBA HRM students. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Deepika
items.liked: The Generative AI course in Sem 1 changed how I handle HR reporting. I now use AI tools for employee survey analysis and communication drafts. Industrial Relations and Labor Laws in Sem 3 was directly applicable to my manufacturing HR role in the Jamshedpur industrial belt.
items.disliked: Only 3 MBA specialisations. A dual-spec option like HRM + Analytics would have been ideal.
items.body: For East India HR professionals, this is a strong option. The JGI Group connection, NAAC A accreditation, and Rs 1.08 lakh fee make it practical. The GenAI Sem 1 subject is a real differentiator. I use those skills daily. The HRM curriculum covers the full lifecycle from talent acquisition to compensation design.

items.name: Manoj
items.liked: Performance Management Systems and Organization Development and Change Management in Sem 4 were strong capstone subjects. Compensation and Benefits Management covered CTC structuring at a practical level.
items.disliked: Brand recognition outside Jharkhand and Bihar is limited. When I mention ARKA JAIN to colleagues in Bengaluru, they assume it is JAIN Deemed University. You have to clarify every time.
items.body: The curriculum is well-designed for the price. Employee Management and Negotiation Skills was a subject I have not seen in other budget MBA programmes. The main trade-off is brand visibility. In East India, the JGI name is familiar. Pan-India, you will need to explain the university in professional settings.

items.name: Priyanka
items.liked: Affordable at Rs 1.08 lakh. Learning and Development subject in Sem 3 covered e-learning strategy and training needs analysis, which was relevant to my L&D coordinator role.
items.disliked: Placement support is limited to East India companies. If you are targeting pan-India HR roles at large corporates, the career services team cannot match what Amity or Manipal offer. LMS could be more polished.
items.body: For the price and accreditation, it works. But be honest about what a sub-Rs 1.1L online MBA from Jharkhand delivers in terms of career acceleration outside East India. The curriculum is good. The brand is the constraint.

faqs.question: Is ARKA JAIN different from JAIN Deemed University?
faqs.answer: Yes. ARKA JAIN University is a separate Jharkhand-based institution in Jamshedpur, operated under the JGI Group. It is NOT the same as JAIN Deemed-to-be University in Bengaluru, which is a different university with different management, different NAAC grade (A++), and different NIRF ranking. Confirm you are applying to the correct institution before making any payment.

faqs.question: What is the Generative AI subject in Sem 1?
faqs.answer: Generative AI for Online Learners is a mandatory credit-bearing subject in Semester 1 across all ARKA JAIN MBA specialisations. For HRM students, this translates to applied skills in AI-assisted recruitment screening, automated HR reporting, prompt engineering for employee communications, and GenAI tools for HR analytics. This is built into the curriculum from the ground up, not a workshop or add-on.

faqs.question: Does ARKA JAIN offer zero-cost EMI?
faqs.answer: Yes. Zero-cost EMI is available directly through ARKA JAIN University. No external NBFC or marketing partner is involved. Contact our counsellor at /contact for current EMI tenure options and monthly instalment amounts.

faqs.question: Does the HRM specialisation cover Indian labour laws?
faqs.answer: Yes. Industrial Relations and Labor Laws is a standalone Semester 3 subject covering the Industrial Disputes Act, Factories Act, new Labour Codes (2020), trade union management, collective bargaining, PF/ESI compliance, and workplace safety norms. This is particularly relevant for HR professionals in manufacturing, mining, and industrial sectors.

faqs.question: What HR roles can I target after this MBA?
faqs.answer: Entry-level roles include HR Executive, Talent Acquisition Specialist, and L&D Coordinator. Mid-level targets include HR Manager, HR Business Partner, and Compensation Manager. Senior roles include Head of HR and CHRO (mid-market companies). The JGI Group network provides stronger access to East India employers. Pan-India or metro-city career targets require independent networking alongside university career support.

chooseIf: Rs 1,08,500 all-inclusive fee with a transparent breakdown (tuition + registration + exam fees) fits your budget.
Industrial Relations and Labor Laws as a standalone subject matters for your manufacturing or industrial HR role.
Mandatory Generative AI subject in Sem 1 gives you applied AI skills for HR analytics and recruitment.
You are based in East India (Jharkhand, Bihar, Odisha, West Bengal) where the JGI Group brand carries recognition.
consider: National brand recognition matters. Amity, NMIMS, and Manipal have stronger pan-India MBA positioning at higher fee points.
SMU at Rs 1,20,000 is only Rs 11,500 more with established Manipal Group brand and national placement infrastructure.
Uttaranchal at Rs 94,000 is Rs 14,500 cheaper with NAAC A+ accreditation.
If your employer requires NIRF top-30, ARKA JAIN (not ranked in NIRF 2025) will not meet that threshold.
